tasks.name: Task 1: Export module with JSON/Markdown/CSV + Export subcommand
tasks.notes: Created export.rs with three format functions. JSON collects all messages then serializes with to_writer_pretty. Markdown streams per-batch with truncation for tool blocks. CSV uses csv crate for proper escaping. All write to Vec<u8> buffer inside conn.call, flushed to stdout outside. Uses tokio_rusqlite::rusqlite::Connection (not direct rusqlite). Added csv dep to server Cargo.toml.

tasks.name: Task 2: VersionCheck + SchemaDrift subcommands
tasks.notes: Added VersionCheck and SchemaDrift variants to Commands enum, completing all 8 Phase 2 subcommands. version-check shows version strings with first/last seen dates. schema-drift shows overflow fields with post-retrieval record_type filter and limit. Both support --json output. query functions were already implemented in Plan 02-02.

decisions.decision: Export functions use tokio_rusqlite::rusqlite::Connection re-export, not direct rusqlite dependency, consistent with server crate's dependency graph
Export writes to Vec<u8> buffer inside conn.call closure, flushed to stdout outside — avoids blocking DB connection thread with I/O per plan specification
SchemaDrift record_type filter applied in Rust post-retrieval — keeps query.rs simple, acceptable since drift entries are typically few

deviations.description: Used tokio_rusqlite::rusqlite::Connection instead of direct rusqlite::Connection — server crate does not have rusqlite as a direct dependency, only through tokio-rusqlite re-export. Also required reborrow (&mut *writer) for serde_json::to_writer_pretty to avoid move-after-borrow on the generic Write impl.
